pr.title: Remove hardcoded strings and use inlined function in aobj.c
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned up src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c by deleting local hardcoded assertion string symbols and replacing an open-coded ref_count increment/assert sequence with the existing inline helper ref_INC(hsd_obj). The change reduced duplicated object reference-count logic while preserving the same assertion behavior through the helper.
what_changed: Removed two file-scope character arrays in aobj.c: lbl_80405FB8 containing "object.h" and lbl_80405FC4 containing "HSD_OBJ(o)->ref_count != HSD_OBJ_NOREF".
In HSD_AObjLoadDesc, replaced a nested hsd_obj != NULL check plus manual ref_count increment and __assert call with a single ref_INC(hsd_obj) call.
Net diff was small and targeted: 1 line added, 11 lines deleted, one changed source file.
smart_moves: Recognized that the open-coded reference-count increment was equivalent to an existing inline/helper path and used ref_INC instead of preserving duplicated logic.
Removed hardcoded assertion string storage that only existed to support the manual __assert call, reducing stray lbl_80405FB8/lbl_80405FC4-style artifacts.
Kept the NULL guard around hsd_obj before calling ref_INC, matching the previous effective control flow without retaining the redundant inner NULL check.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled function manually increments HSD_Obj.ref_count and checks for HSD_OBJ_NOREF, look for the canonical helper or inline, here ref_INC, before accepting local duplicated code.
Hardcoded string labels such as lbl_80405FB8 and lbl_80405FC4 can be symptoms of an uninlined assertion path; replacing the surrounding logic with the intended helper can eliminate those labels.
Small cleanup PRs can improve source quality and decomp readability even when they do not expand matching coverage or introduce new symbols.
Preserve semantic guards around helper calls: this PR retained the outer hsd_obj != NULL condition and only removed the redundant nested check.
naming_conventions: Prefer semantic helper names such as ref_INC over generated label/string names like lbl_80405FB8 and lbl_80405FC4 when the helper is known.
Use existing HSD/Baselib object terminology consistently: HSD_Obj, ref_count, HSD_OBJ_NOREF, HSD_AObjLoadDesc, HSD_IDGetDataFromTable, HSD_JObjLoadJoint.
Generated lbl_-address names should be removed when they are only supporting artifacts for code that can be expressed with established project helpers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace an open-coded sequence of taking &hsd_obj->ref_count, incrementing it, comparing against (u16)-1, and calling __assert with ref_INC(hsd_obj) when this matches the compiler's inlined/helper behavior.
Eliminating the explicit __assert arguments can remove the need for local string symbols while relying on the helper's canonical assertion implementation.
Redundant nested NULL checks may appear in decompiled output; if the outer guard already dominates the helper call, the inner check can often be collapsed when matching permits.
follow_up_queries: Find other baselib files with manual HSD_Obj ref_count increments that could use ref_INC.
Search for remaining hardcoded assertion string labels for object.h or HSD_OBJ(o)->ref_count != HSD_OBJ_NOREF.
Audit redundant nested NULL checks around HSD_IDGetDataFromTable results in decompiled baselib code.
